name: Chhath Puja
traditions: Standing in rivers at dawn and dusk offering prayers to the Sun God; fasting for 36 hours; making offerings of fruit and thekua.
historicalBackground: Chhath Puja is among the oldest Hindu festivals, dedicated to Surya (Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya. It is unique to Bihar, Jharkhand, and eastern UP.
description: Bihar's ancient sun worship festival, where millions stand in rivers at dawn to offer prayers in one of Hinduism's most austere rituals.
